sanskrit:
लभन्ते ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृषयः क्षीणकल्मषाः।
छिन्नद्वैधा यतात्मानः सर्वभूतहिते रताः॥
transliteration:
labhante brahma-nirvāṇam ṛṣayaḥ kṣīṇa-kalmaṣāḥ |
chinna-dvaidhā yatātmānaḥ sarva-bhūta-hite ratāḥ ||
literal_meaning: They attain Brahman-liberation—seers whose stains are worn away, whose duality is cut, whose selves are yoked, delighting in the welfare of all beings.
contextual_meaning: Lasting inner peace is reached when the mind is freed from inner conflict, self-centered fear, and the split between ‘me’ and ‘them’, and is redirected toward universal care.
psychological_state: Post-ambivalent integration: cognitive fusion (dvaidha) resolved, self-boundaries relaxed, empathic concern generalized to all beings, guilt-free self-concept.
trigger_scenario: A burnt-out social worker realizes that rescuing others while resenting them keeps her stuck; she starts boundary-setting self-care and sees clients as equals, suddenly feeling energized and guilt-free.
anchor_text: Work for everyone’s welfare without selfish division.
